Class Move

java.lang.Object
model.Move
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
Direct Known Subclasses:
Blizzard, Covet, DracoMeteor, Earthquake, Facade, FireFang, Flamethrower, Glaciate, HeadSmash, HyperFang, IcePunch, PoisonJab, RazorLeaf, SludgeWave, StoneEdge, VineWhip, Waterfall, WildboltStorm
public abstract class Move extends Object implements Serializable
Abstract class that all Mon moves should inherit from. Defines the basic requirements for what a move should do, but leaves its effect to be implemented by the move itself.
Author:
Isa Abdulrazaq
See Also:

    • getPower

      Getter for power
      Returns:
      int power

    • applyEffect

      public void applyEffect(Mon user, Mon enemy)
      Abstract method that must be implemented by all subclassing moves. Defines what the move actually does and how it changes the game state
      Parameters:
      user - the Mon using the move. Useful if the move buffs the user in any way.
      enemy - the Mon battling the move's user. Will call
      invalid reference 
      Mon#takeDamage(int, Mon)
      if the move is a damaging move.

    • getPP

      public int getPP()
      Used to ensure that a move isn't usable if it no longer has PP
      Returns:
      PP the number of uses remaining for the move

    • getMaxPP

      public int getMaxPP()
      Returns the maxPP value for the UI
      Returns:
      maxPP The total number of times the move can be used.

    • refillPP

      public void refillPP()
      Sets PP to maxPP

    • updatePP

      public void updatePP()
      Updates PP by deducting PP by 1
